programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. The bombing campaign continues remorselessly, but Dr Sloan thinks he may be closing in on the perpetrator. Crime drama, starring Dick and Barry Van Dyke, with Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Sloan strikes a bargain with Caitlin Sweeney's imprisoned brother in an attempt to bring the bomber to justice. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Part two of two. Dr Sloan strikes a bargain with Caitlin Sweeney's imprisoned brother in an attempt to bring the bomber to justice. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke and Barry Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The bodies of five people, all of whom are identified as being from the US Navy, are found on the floor of a diner, and all are connected to a suspicious tax firm that specialises in helping people who are in arrears. When further killings are linked to the business, the team realises the crime seem to be copycats of previous cases. Meanwhile, two of Gibbs' ex-wives appear at the location with something important to discuss with the agent. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: American social worker Sarah Goode is taken hostage by militants in northern Syria, and thanks to the efforts of the Navy Seals, is eventually returned home unharmed. Evidence from the incident suggests US citizen Kyle Nasry was connected to the kidnapping, but when the agents trace Nasry to a rally held by a controversial imam, they are surprised to find Sarah among the attendees. Meanwhile, DiNozzo finally takes his relationship with Zoe Keates public - surprising none of his colleagues. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and his colleagues investigate when Eric Kutzler, the first openly gay Navy lieutenant tipped to receive the Medal of Honour, is murdered. While the agents initially suspect Kutzler's death was the result of a hate crime, the fact that his police officer husband waited 20 minutes to report the crime suggests he had something to hide. Meanwhile, the team throws a baby shower for Palmer as his wife's due date approaches. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo investigates a murder at the military academy he once attended, after a murdered marine and alumni of the school is found clutching a photo of a current student. Meanwhile, Bishop and her husband invite the team out for a special dinner. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. Meanwhile, Paige considers staying in Los Angeles or moving 3,000 miles away to Portland, Maine, so Ralph can attend an exclusive school. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team searches for stolen experimental shoulder-launch rockets, which are rumoured to being transported on a luxury cruise ship. Meanwhile, Ralph impresses his first crush with the help of Happy and Toby. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Teenager Lydia Wade manages to escape a gang of masked men trying to abduct her and her friend Rosie, the daughter of a US Navy commander. Lydia's Navy captain father David Wade was shot dead during the attack and her mother is overseas, so Ziva offers to look after the girl, while Abby tries to jog her memory by creating facial reconstructions of the attackers based on some grainy CCTV footage. DiNozzo and McGee find links on Rosie's laptop to possible suspect John 'Diesel' Stevens

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is called to the scene when there is an attempt on the life of FBI agent TC Fornell, with the assailant identified as Navy seaman Tyler Brown. The pair team up and visit the bar where Brown worked and bump into their mutual ex-wife Diane Sterling, who was operating undercover as an employee suspected of stealing customers' identities. Joe Spano and Melinda McGraw guest star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Returning from a six-month posting in the Gulf, Navy lieutenant commander Megan Huffner opens her front door to find her husband Noel dead in his chair. Gibbs and the team investigate and find the victim died three or four weeks earlier, and an unusual $100 bill in his wallet proves to be a vital clue. Meanwhile, Tony's father visits for Christmas and looks to rebuild their fractured relationship. Guest starring Robert Wagner

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man and his son on a fishing trip hook what appears to be the body of a petty officer, but the dead man turns out to be a journalist who had been investigating the proliferation of drugs in the Navy. Meanwhile, Ziva returns to her car after a workout at the gym to find her father Eli hiding inside. Guest starring Michael Nouri

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. After the deaths of two family members of NCIS employees, the team identifies the killer as a Swedish mercenary and the ballistics report reveals the bullets came from an Iranian arms dealer. McGee traces a wire transfer made to the hitman's ex-wife and son to an account under the name Virtue, and Ziva makes a connection after realising what the Hebrew translation is

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team enlists a former secret service agent to help try to prevent a nuclear attack on an American ally, after an attempted hacker tries to launch a weapon of mass destruction. programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Part two of two. O'Neill heads to Texas to apprehend Eddie Lomax, whom he believes to be the real killer. Matlock realises that a land scam is guiding events, and enlists an unlikely ally - the victim's wife. Guest starring Dennis Franz (NYPD Blue)

programme.title: JAG
programme.desc: Harm is arrested by the FBI and charged with murdering a member of the Russian Mafia who claimed to have information about the detective's father's fate, while Mac faces the challenge of adjusting to civilian life. David James Elliott stars

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: A drunken woman vanishes in the water forcing the boys to call in the helicopter to help find her. Terry has the most critical rescue of the season, which also brings back some emotional memories for him

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: An engaged couple meet the night before their wedding to draw up a cunning plan to murder the bride-to-be's father. Medical detective drama, featuring a guest appearance by former Dallas star Patrick Duffy

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: A kidnapper tries to call his victim's family to demand a hefty ransom - but dials Dr Sloan's number by mistake, sowing the seeds of his own downfall. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Reports of a man's death turn out to be somewhat exaggerated, and a reign of terror ensues at Community General as Dr Sloan tries to discover the identity of the seemingly resurrected maniac. Guest starring Bryan Cranston

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A billionaire hires the team to help when his daughter is kidnapped. Meanwhile, Walter upsets the gang when he is interviewed and fails to acknowledge the other members. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps to facilitate secret peace talks to prevent a third world war when a conflict in Eastern Europe threatens to escalate. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: CIA agent Trent Kort returns to track down dangerous criminal Jonathan Siravo and Gibbs is forced to put his personal feelings aside to assist in the investigation. The duo manage to get hold of Siravo's accountant, who agrees to reveal all he knows in exchange for full immunity, but the trail leads to an old man in a coma in what seems to be a case of stolen identity

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An army scientist goes missing, so Abby is asked to temporarily replace him on a top-secret research project - but Gibbs later learns her assignment is connected to a current murder investigation, and worries she may meet the same fate as her predecessor. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. When the body of a marine is found strung up at a construction site, the agents travel to LA to work with another NCIS team on an investigation into an international arms deal that escalates into a high-priority case, and Tony begins to pry into Ziva's life. Guest starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J (NCIS: Los Angeles)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Agent Callen poses as an arms trade contact in a bid to catch killer Michael Aaron Rivkin. Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is forced to work alongside the FBI to catch the killer of an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent who was murdered during a poker game at the Secretary of the Navy's mansion. Elsewhere, Tony takes on Rivkin in a confrontation that ends in one of them being shot

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: A billionaire hires the team to help when his daughter is kidnapped. Meanwhile, Walter upsets the gang when he is interviewed and fails to acknowledge the other members. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team helps to facilitate secret peace talks to prevent a third world war when a conflict in Eastern Europe threatens to escalate. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Jadyn Wong

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A woman is shot dead in an apartment while keeping watch on a man on a yacht in a marina, and the killer takes her camera and computer. A flash drive is recovered from the victim's baseball cap and surveillance footage on it includes images of Callen, who deduces that the pictures were taken in the days leading up to the attempt on his life and that the dead woman was the last person to see him before he was shot

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam meet up with former colleague Mike Renko, who is back in town on the trail of a civilian militia he thinks is responsible for the theft of anti-tank missiles from a Navy base. Their discussion is interrupted by Hetty with footage of a man being strangled at an aquarium - and the victim is identified as a lance corporal who was Renko's inside man at the group

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate the murder of an engineer working on highly sensitive submarine technology, but find the crime scene clean of evidence. Forensic scientist Abby Sciuto travels from the NCIS office in Washington, DC to help out as the case has similarities to a serial killer she has been tracking. Guest starring Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen and Sam investigate when former marine Asher Ross is killed by an explosive device hidden in his mobile phone. Because of the sophisticated nature of the bomb, suspicion falls on the members of the ordnance disposal unit Ross was part of in Iraq, but further enquiries reveal that they are being targeted as well

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A Navy officer dies following a confrontation involving a dancer and a Sudanese emigre at a strip club. However, when investigations reveal he was a code-breaker involved in naval intelligence, Callen and Sam dig deeper and gradually unearth links to Safar, a Libyan-born terrorist known to recruit on behalf of al-Qaeda. Henri Lubatti guest stars

programme.desc: Bud temporarily moves in with Harriet, while Harm investigates the case of a Navy ensign who admits to shooting a Turkish military attache, but claims she acted in self-defence. David James Elliott stars

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

Abby calls in sick for the first time after the case stirs up a traumatic incident from her childhood. Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette star

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Dan Stanwyck, an investigator working on a report into police corruption, is fatally shot the night before his findings are published. Taylor and Stella are convinced he was killed by a crooked officer whose wrongdoings were about to be exposed. Meanwhile, Danny and Aiden look into the case of a taxi driver stabbed to death in his cab

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella investigate a hotel frequented by foreign diplomats after a woman's body is found wrapped in bedsheets. They tangle with a trade representative whose murky past suggests he could be the killer. Meanwhile, Danny argues with his boss over the corpse of a homeless man found covered with silver paint. Crime drama, starring Gary Sinise, Melina Kanakaredes and Carmine Giovinazzo

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Taylor investigates the murder of a student who has been beaten and shot, and the discovery of heroin traces in his apartment leads him to suspect the killing was drug-related. The case becomes more urgent when it emerges that the victim's female room-mate has disappeared. Stella embarks on a harsh line of interrogation that results in her methods being called into question

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A routine investigation at a crime scene turns sour when Danny is attacked by a criminal hiding in the victim's house. He gives chase to the subway, where shots are fired - during which the offender vanishes and a police officer is killed, leaving Danny as prime suspect. Meanwhile, Stella investigates the discovery of a nanny's body in a Central Park public lavatory. Drama, starring Gary Sinise and Melina Kanakaredes

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: A murder suspect's claims of innocence shake Mac's confidence and make him doubt the validity of prosecution evidence. Meanwhile, a link emerges between the cases of a scantily clad woman hit by a lorry and a baseball fan who died after the defeat of his favourite team. programme.desc: Sandy has relationship problems and Jean frets when Judith starts dating a married man. At a dinner party attended by Penny and Stephen, Lionel has to steer the conversation away from personal matters. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean and Lionel's peaceful weekend in the country is disrupted when they find the faithful Mrs Bale is not her usual cheerful self. To add to their woes, Lionel feels unwell - but a visit from a doctor with a strange bedside manner sheds light on their housekeeper's mood

programme.title: Murder on the Blackpool Express
programme.desc: Feature-length comedy about a crime writer (Griff Rhys Jones) who takes a group of his fans on a coach tour of locations from his books. programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Jared discovers a severed human hand in a crayfish pot, begging the questions who does it belong to and are they still alive? Detective drama, starring Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Tracking a local conman, Frank and Lu make some unlikely allies when they encounter a pair of deadly gangsters. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives who investigate crime in Stratford-upon-Avon

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The detective oversteps the mark and finds himself sent back to the Police Training College to learn some professional etiquette and save his pension. Sidekick DS Siobhan Clarke is left to investigate the murder of an art dealer, but it is not long before her boss turns up on the trail of the killer. Ken Stott stars in the crime drama, with Claire Price and Jennifer Black

programme.title: Wallander
programme.desc: Two bodies are discovered on a life raft in Ystad harbour, the victims having been shot through the head and had their tongues burnt off with acid. The heavily tattooed corpses reveal links to the Russian mob and are instrumental in proving the men originated from Latvia, so Major Karlis Liepa, an officer with the country's police department, is sent to work with Wallander. But an anonymous phone call, a shooting and a break-in at the police HQ soon convince Wallander of a leak in the investigation - and he immediately suspects Liepa. Guest starring Soren Malling (The Killing), with Kenneth Branagh as the world-weary detective

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: An ageing confectionery company owner is found dead in his London hotel suite, and while Nikki tries to convince the sceptical DS Gold he was murdered, several businessmen begin to circle as it turns out the dead man was losing a desperate fight to prevent the sale of his company to investors. Feature-length episode of the crime drama, starring Emilia Fox, William Gaminara and David Caves, with guest appearances by Tim Pigott-Smith, Sharon Small and Priyanga Burford

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene is convinced a bomb warning in Manchester is part of a campaign by the IRA and plans to investigate the Irish locals. The team's respect for Sam is shaken when his 21st-century investigation methods put them at risk, but he insists the link is too obvious and tries to open his boss's eyes to the benefits of good community relations

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. A couple return from a night away to discover their baby has been abducted from her bed. The child's nanny - the last person to see her - gives a garbled account of events, covering for her boyfriend who had visited on the night in question. But as Tony gently interrogates the parents, another suspect emerges

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The baby's body is found and evidence stacks up against the nanny's boyfriend, but Sam's gentle interrogation of the parents produces another startling suspect. Eventually, toxicology reports on the victim present a stark truth demanding all the DI's sensitivity and tact as she helps the grieving mother face up to what happened

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. When a talented young athlete is found battered to death in a park, officers interrogate fellow guests at a house party he had attended earlier that evening. His schoolmates suggest gatecrashers bringing drugs into the house may have been responsible, but their versions of events reveal inconsistencies and suggest a completely different explanation

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Heaton and Meadows play a psychological game, putting pressure on the teenage partygoers by staging a reconstruction and holding a press conference to rattle the composure of the suspects - aiming to uncover the truth about the death of the young athlete. Eddie finds a piece of wood which he believes is the murder weapon, but the nail that caused the puncture wound in the victim's spine is missing

programme.title: North and South
programme.desc: Four-part adaptation of Elizabeth Gaskell's novel following the fortunes of Margaret Hale, a privileged middle-class southerner who is forced to settle in the northern town of Milton. Over time she grows accustomed to her new surroundings and her social conscience awakens as she befriends local mill workers, and discovers the poverty and workplace struggles they face. Daniela Denby-Ashe, Lesley Manville and Sinead Cusack star

programme.title: North and South
programme.desc: Thornton's family questions Margaret's intentions when she intervenes in an industrial dispute at the mill. Maria's health continues to deteriorate, and desperate to see her son Frederick one last time, she pens a letter to him in Spain, begging for his return. Drama based on the novel by Elizabeth Gaskell, starring Daniela Denby-Ashe and Richard Armitage

programme.title: North and South
programme.desc: Margaret is drawn to Thornton despite adopting a steely resolve to aid the workers in their battle after tragedy strikes. The arrival of an unexpected visitor brings relief to Maria, whose condition has gradually deteriorated, and sparks a chain of events which causes Thornton to reach the wrong conclusion

programme.title: North and South
programme.desc: Margaret is forced to move away from Milton after tragedy strikes. However, when her godfather leaves her a substantial inheritance she makes a return to the North, where a twist of fate leads to an unexpected rendezvous

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Dwelling Place
programme.desc: Feature-length adaptation of the best-selling novel, telling the tale of a 16-year-old struggling to keep her five brothers and sisters out of the workhouse with the help of a kindly carpenter. Starring James Fox, Tracy Whitwell and Edward Rawle-Hicks

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Rumours spread around Poplar that a sailor with smallpox is hiding somewhere in town. Nurse Crane and Dr Turner try to track him down before he causes an outbreak. Lucille treats an expectant mother on her own, but soon feels out of her depth when she displays signs of a serious medical condition. Trixie goes to stay with her godmother, Violet and Fred organise a picnic for the parish, and Tom and Barbara return home

programme.title: Miss Marple
programme.desc: A film star arrives from America and throws a summer garden party for the entire village of St Mary Mead - where one of her fans drinks a poisoned cocktail and promptly drops dead. Fortunately, the guest list includes Miss Marple, who immediately sets about tracking down the killer. Feature-length drama, starring Joan Hickson, Claire Bloom and Barry Newman

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Burke and the team investigate university lecturer and crime novelist Mark Joffe, whose debut publication includes the detailed description of a murder identical to that of a real-life unsolved case. As the case unfolds, Burke becomes convinced of the writer's guilt, but cannot seem to find sufficient evidence to charge him. Guest starring Finlay Robertson (Life Begins), with Alex Norton, Blythe Duff and John Michie

programme.title: Murder on the Blackpool Express
programme.desc: Feature-length comedy about a crime writer (Griff Rhys Jones) who takes a group of his fans on a coach tour of locations from his books. The bus is soon leaving a string of bodies in its wake, and the passengers are faced with the possibility of a murderer in their midst. With Johnny Vegas, Nigel Havers and Una Stubbs

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Gene fears a serial killer he brought to justice in the 1960s has resurfaced after a woman's body is found. The victim's resemblance to a family member triggers childhood memories for Sam, who goes undercover in middle-class suburbia to investigate. Drama, guest starring former Coronation Street actresses Eva Pope and Georgia Taylor, with Philip Glenister and John Simm

programme.title: Life on Mars
programme.desc: Sam faces a race against time as he attempts to forget his troubles and focus on an investigation into the kidnapping of a man's wife and daughter. As he picks through the details of the crime, he begins to consider who may be motivated to hold the pair hostage - only for Annie to leave herself exposed when she discovers the kidnapper's true identity. Fantasy drama, starring John Simm and Philip Glenister

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of three. Human remains found buried in a park turn out to be those of a student who went missing 20 years previously. Suspicion immediately falls on her boyfriend and a couple they were close to in their teens. The case brings Heaton face to face with an old adversary, raising the possibility that the man he sent down for murder in the 1980s may in fact be a serial killer

programme.desc: Cyd is bitten by a venomous snake inadvertently transported from Peru, prompting Dixie to finally declare her true feelings as the team fight to save their colleague's life. programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of three. The discovery of two more bodies convinces Heaton his suspect is a serial killer, but the man he sent to prison 20 years before sticks to his story that he is innocent. Bad Girls' James Gaddas guest stars

programme.desc: The Jackson family welcomes Billy home but must decide whether to move to another area for safety. Tony visits the Jacksons to get the story for the Gazette

Meanwhile, a care-home cancer patient receives a chilling gift from a visitor, and three girls look forward to a night in - unaware of the danger lurking in their home. programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day six of the tournament at the All England Club, where third-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played. Top seed Ashleigh Barty had too much for British wildcard Harriet Dart at this stage when the tournament was last played in 2019, prevailing 6-1, 6-1. Another British player to suffer disappointment was Dan Evans, who lost in five sets to Joao Sousa, while Andy Murray experienced mixed fortunes, winning alongside Serena Williams in the mixed doubles after he and Pierre-Hugues Herbert had gone out of the men's doubles

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Ukraine v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the concluding quarter-final, held at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, as the winners of the seventh and eighth last-16 matches battle it out. Gareth Southgate's men finally ended their tournament hoodoo against Germany on Tuesday, scoring twice in the last 15 minutes at Wembley to defeat their old enemy in a knockout game for the first time since the 1966 World Cup Final. Raheem Sterling notched his third goal of the tournament to send the crowd into raptures, and captain Harry Kane finished the job with a neat header from Jack Grealish's cross. Ukraine progressed thanks to a 2-1 victory over Sweden at Hampden Park, with Artem Dovbyk striking in added time in extra time

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a high-energy house hunt in the Norfolk countryside where he helps a dynamic couple quit urban life in London and find a character family home for their £400,000 budget

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
programme.desc: A hero of ancient Greece vows to save a princess from being sacrificed to a ferocious sea monster. However, to defeat the creature he must first embark on a journey into the underworld and seize the head of the monstrous Medusa, whose gaze turns men to stone. Mythical adventure, starring Harry Hamlin, Laurence Olivier, Maggie Smith and Judi Bowker, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison celebrate the 150th anniversary of Wimbledon Common, encountering the horseback patrols that enforce the Common's 57 bylaws and discovering how the Wombles have inspired a whole new generation of ecologists. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to protect the world's oceans are delivering for UK waters, and Dwayne Fields leads four youngsters at a crossroad in their lives on an expedition into the Welsh wilderness

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.title: Molly's Game (2017)
programme.desc: The true story of Molly Bloom, an Olympic-class skier who ran the world's most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested in the middle of the night by FBI agents wielding automatic weapons. Her players included Hollywood royalty, sports stars, business titans and finally - unbeknownst to her - the Russian Mob. Drama directed by The West Wing creator Aaron Sorkin, starring Jessica Chastain, Idris Elba and Kevin Costner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a notorious case of supposed matricide dating from the 1920s, which may have resulted in an innocent man being hanged

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Ross presents the show from Brackley, Northamptonshire, and discovers how old food can be collectable. With experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day seven of the tournament at the All England Club, where fourth-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta delighted the home crowd when she came from a set down to defeat two-time champion Petra Kvitova at this stage two years ago. She was followed on Centre Court by Roger Federer, who was in dominant form as he overwhelmed 17th seed Matteo Berrettini 6-1, 6-2, 6-2

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and David and Harry Rich come up with competing designs for a Bristol couple on a budget of £6,500, The couple want to transform their large, disordered and overgrown garden into an urban suntrap reminiscent of their holidays in the Mediterranean

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood introduce another selection of clips, this time featuring pets, including a trip to a rabbit island, and a rat that chases cats

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Daisy needs a sewing den where she can create fabulous designs in peace

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Trying to win Lauren's shiny gold stars are team captains Olivia and Nayan

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The cast of Almost Never are live in the BP studio with fun and games and an exclusive performance

programme.title: What's Cooking Omari?
programme.desc: Young cook Omari makes plum blackberry crumble as a surprise for his mum

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve discovers just what it takes to be a terror from the skies. He straps himself into one of Britain's fastest wind tunnels where he goes head to head against the bird-like Microraptor. He also considers what was possibly the largest flier ever - Hatzegopteryx

programme.title: Human Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox concludes his exploration of humanity's place in the cosmos by examining what the future holds. In Florida, he learns about the latest efforts to protect Earth from potential catastrophic events and joins a team of Nasa astronauts who are in a submerged laboratory that simulates space as they train for a future mission to an asteroid - should one ever be discovered heading to Earth. At the National Ignition Facility in California, the physicist witnesses the world's most successful fusion experiment in action, one which he believes may unlock a way to the stars that will not destroy the planet in the process

programme.title: Mountain: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: The extraordinary animals and people who make their homes in mountain ranges around the world, beginning with the Rockies. The mountains are the spine of North America, a beautiful wilderness of snowcapped peaks and hidden valleys, home to many animals including cougars, wolves and grizzly bears

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day six of the tournament at the All England Club, where the third round is scheduled to conclude in the men's and women's singles. Rafael Nadal did not take part in this year's competition, but produced a strong display at this stage in 2019 to comfortably defeat the often-dangerous Jo-Wilfried Tsonga 6-2, 6-3, 6-2. There were also victories for two players who have won an incredible 15 singles titles between them, with Roger Federer winning Lucas Pouille 7-5, 6-2, 7-6, and Serena Williams prevailing 6-3, 6-4 against Julia Gorges, though both players eventually ended the tournament as runners-up two years ago, losing the finals to Novak Djokovic and Simona Halep respectively. Another former champion was also in good form in round three, with 2011 and 2014 winner Petra Kvitova winning 6-3, 6-2 against Magda Linette

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the sixth day's play at the All England Club, where the line-up for the last 16 was set to be decided. This first Saturday of the tournament is always one of the busiest days and will have been eagerly anticipated, even more so after the competition did not take place last year due to the Covid-19 pandemic

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Part one of two. Celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Fern Brady, Desiree Burch, Alan Carr, Larry Dean, Eddie Izzard, Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons

programme.title: Pride Hits at the BBC
programme.desc: A musical celebration for International Pride Month, with a collection of the BBC's archives' biggest stars, including Kylie, Elton John and Lady Gaga, along with once-controversial tracks such as Relax and Smalltown Boy

programme.title: Pride (2014)
programme.desc: Gay rights activists gather in London for a pride march in 1984, and decide they have a common cause with the miners' strike. When their fundraising activities are rejected by the miners' union, they travel to a Welsh pit village to show their support in person. Fact-based drama, starring Ben Schnetzer, Bill Nighy, Andrew Scott and Imelda Staunton

programme.title: Summertime (2015)
programme.desc: An ambitious farmer's daughter travels to Paris in 1971, where she falls in love with a radical feminist. She finds herself obliged to return to the family farm, and her new lover accompanies her on the journey, but their relationship is greeted with disapproval. Romantic drama, starring Cecile De France and Izia Higelin. In French

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: Winter has arrived and harsh frosts have shattered the tulip pots and ruined the brick paths, so Carol takes shelter in the potting shed and prepares her garlic for spring planting. On finer days, she begins pruning late-flowering clematis, and begins a major revamp in the borders when the thaw comes. While the trees and shrubs are still in their dormancy in February, a local woodsman arrives to lay a native hedge

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin heads to the Yorkshire Dales to meet the Pateley Bridge Bloomers, who are determined to save their local high street after it was affected by dwindling tourist numbers

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Adam Frost adds tall plants for shady conditions to his borders, shows how to make a window box for growing vegetables and herbs and gives tips on how to grow plants successfully in peat-free compost. Nick Bailey visits Kew Gardens to explore their collection of ferns and cameras return to the urban garden of Kate Bradbury to find out how she ensures that visiting wildlife is kept well fed throughout the summer months. Plus, the Isle of Man garden where a range of exotic plants are thriving in the microclimate on the island and a nurseryman in Kent with a passion for hydrangeas

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Cumbrian Hills, and catch up with a farming mother and son who are diversifying to make ends meet. Charlotte also reports on efforts to protect the surrounding landscape. Plus, Joe Crowley investigates a worrying rise in attacks on livestock by dogs off the leash

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the 17th-century harbour of Portsoy in Aberdeenshire, to find out how the Scottish Traditional Boat Festival has gone online and takes a trip on a 1960s fishing boat with a colourful history

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter and Mairi Rattray take a look at the progress of the beds of wildflower seeds and turf that they sowed and laid eight weeks ago. Kirsty Wilson is also at Beechgrove and she and Carole and Mairi enjoy a masterclass on the Japanese art of Kokedama, or creating Japanese moss balls, with expert Fiona McDonald from Aberdeen

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, offering viewers a chance to revisit some of their favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Country House Secrets
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, Mary visits Goodwood House, home to the March family, famous for their iconic horse racing and motoring events. She is taken on a thrilling lap of Goodwood motor circuit by Lord March, before attending one of his infamous dinner parties where 300 guests dine in style in splendid state rooms. Mary also learns more about the Estate's organic farm, before preparing a Coq-au-Vin and a four-tier cake for a cricket tea set in one of the oldest cricket clubs in the world

programme.title: Reel History of Britain
programme.desc: Melvyn Bragg's mobile cinema parks alongside the College of Medical and Dental Sciences in Birmingham to highlight the formation of the NHS in 1948. June Rosen shares a childhood memory of delivering breakfast to then-Health Secretary Nye Bevan on the day he introduced the service. A leading nurse discusses arriving from Barbados to begin her training, and the show hears from the woman who was the first baby to be born on the NHS

programme.title: Into the Woods (2014)
programme.desc: A wicked witch agrees to lift a curse on a young couple if they will help restore her once beautiful looks. Musical fantasy, based on Stephen Sondheim's hit stage show, which entwines the fairy tale stories of Cinderella, Little Red Riding Hood, Jack and the Beanstalk and Rapunzel. Starring Meryl Streep, James Corden and Emily Blunt, with a cameo by Johnny Depp as the Big Bad Wolf

programme.title: Live Athletics
programme.desc: The BAUHAUS-galan. Coverage of the fifth round of the campaign, held at Stockholm Olympic Stadium in Sweden. British athletes impressed here last year, with Laura Muir coasting to victory in the 1,500m, Adam Gemili winning the 200m and Jemma Reekie finishing way out in front in the 800m. This time around, pole vault world-record holder will be looking to lay down a marker in front of his home crowd ahead of the Olympics

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral, once home to Benedictine monks. Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some items to take to auction. Among them are an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist. Paul also explores the history of the nearby Broads.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Adventurer and explorer Paul Rose heads for Wensleydale as he sets out to explore the region, starting in the small town of Hawes, where locals now run many of their own services, including the buses. Paul checks out why cycling is so popular in the Dales and tries to get to the top of the Buttertubs Pass. Along the way he meets a family of dormice and spends time with a man who looks after one of the area's most imposing castles

programme.title: Africa with Ade Adepitan
programme.desc: Ade concludes his journey by exploring southern Africa. In Mozambique, he discovers that a declining tourist trade has benefited wildlife and explores local attitudes to disability. Moving on to South Africa, he investigates how much life has changed in the 25 years since the downfall of apartheid. His journey ends in Zimbabwe, a country that is still struggling economically despite having vast quantities of gold

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Raymond's bull Major returns to work after being injured earlier in the year, but getting him to the field of cows involves a soggy detour. Raymond also travels to Switzerland with fiancee Jen to help their friends gather their valais blacknose sheep from the mountaintops. Kirsty and Aimee go sheep shopping to double their flock, while bad news forces buffalo farmer Stevie to make a shocking decision

programme.title: Our Wild Adventures
programme.desc: The BBC's favourite wildlife presenters look back through the archives as they share their most memorable adventures in Britain and Ireland. Chris Packham remembers a recent filming trip where he had the privilege to spot some extremely rare birds circling the skies above his home county of Hampshire - white-tailed eagles. Gordon Buchanan recalls joining a fishing trawler to film killer whales off the coast of Shetland, while Vianet Djenguet describes his first sight of a cuckoo in Norfolk and Steve Backshall recalls filming seahorses

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe, Colin Lane and Jimmy Carr join regular panellist Alan Davies and host Sandi Toksvig for an extended edition of the comedy panel quiz show. This time, the theme is 'Opposites'. During the course of the show, the panellists find out how to sort the sheep from the goats, discover the opposite of Tarzan, and meet the telescope that points straight through the Earth. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window, as the fiendishly difficult questions lead to points being awarded for the answers its questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: The return of the crime drama set in the borderlands between Mexico and America. The Mayans and their Californian town suffer as federal forces crack down on all illegal activity, and grieving Galindo lets his legitimate project dry up too

programme.title: Mayans M.C.
programme.desc: Against better judgement, EZ puts a proposal to Bishop, rebel Adelita is handed to the Mexican authorities, and Coco goes gunning for opioids

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Sri Lanka. Action from the third and final match in the three-game series, held at The Bristol County Ground

programme.title: Arctic with Bruce Parry
programme.desc: The adventurer travels to a remote Russian village deep within a forest that stretches for thousands of miles, where people rely on the food they can gather from their surroundings. He then heads to Norway to spend time with the Sami reindeer herders who use snowmobiles, boats and sometimes helicopters to move their animals. His six-month journey through the Arctic concludes with a visit to a scientific community in the Svalbard archipelago, where he learns about the fragility of the ecosystem - before witnessing the aurora borealis, also known as the northern lights

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce introduces the weekly topical debate, with a virtual audience from around the UK. Her panellists are Conservative backbencher and former Home Secretary and Brexit Secretary David Davis MP, Ex-Labour MP, now Mayor of Greater Manchester Andy Burnham, entrepreneur and founder of the African-inspired snack range Chika's Foods Chika Russell, Parliamentary sketch writer at the Daily Telegraph and columnist at the Sunday Telegraph Madeline Grant and poet, playwright and author Benjamin Zephaniah

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Lucky tries to overcome her personal crisis by throwing herself into work. But in her eagerness to go above and beyond she oversteps the mark. A patient accuses Dominic of leaving an unwanted item in his body during an operation, and Kylie put her foot in it with her flatmates

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reexamine a complex case from Victorian Britain that featured four prime suspects, two trials, and ultimately just one man hanged for the murder of his neighbour. In 1882, 43-year old shop-keeper Harriet Candler was killed in a brutal attack, with her property ransacked

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Vintage musical instrument expert Pete Woods takes on a tarnished, dented saxophone that once played to lift people's spirits during the Blitz, but has not made a sound in more than 40 years. Silversmith Brenton West's skills are tested when he is handed an 80-year-old brass kettle in desperate need of professional attention. Saddler Suzie Fletcher restores a leather shaving mirror used by troops in the trenches of the First World War, and metal-worker Dominic Chinea sharpens up his act with a vintage carpenter's fret saw that has seen better days

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Hull Kingston Rovers v Hull FC and Castleford Tigers v St Helens

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of last week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day seven of the tournament at the All England Club, where fourth-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played, with places in the quarter-finals up for grabs. Rafael Nadal was among the victors at this stage in 2019, impressing on Centre Court with a 6-2, 6-2, 6-2 victory over Joao Sousa, but the two-time champion was ruled out of this year's tournament. Over on the No. 1 Court, eventual champion Simona Halep ended the memorable run of then 15-year-old Coco Gauff with a 6-3, 6-3 victory. There were also typically dominant victories for Serena Williams and Novak Djokovic over Carla Suarez Navarro and Ugo Humbert respectively. However, things did not go to plan for top seed Ashleigh Barty on the No.2 Court, as the was defeated in three sets by Alison Riske, before third seed Karolina Pliskova followed her out after losing a gruelling match 4-6, 7-5, 13-11 to Czech compatriot Karolina Muchova

programme.title: Today at Wimbledon
programme.desc: Clare Balding introduces highlights of the seventh day's play at the All England Club, where the line-up for the quarter-finals in the singles draws was set to be decided. This is invariably one of the most exciting days on the schedule, with all of the men's and ladies' fourth-round singles matches set to be played

programme.title: Heist: The Northern Bank Robbery
programme.desc: Darragh MacIntyre and Sam McBride shed new light on the Northern Bank Robbery. Two families were held hostage for 24 hours, while two bank employees were forced to rob £26.5million. Despite an international police investigation and heavily rumoured IRA involvement, the case remains unsolved after 16 years. Darragh and Sam examine internal bank CCTV, police 999 calls, archive footage and court transcripts for fresh clues, and consider the impact of the crime on the peace process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: A Dangerous Dynasty: House of Assad
programme.desc: Documentary about the al-Assad family, which has ruled Syria since 1971 and established an authoritarian government in the country under the control of the Ba'ath Party. Current Syrian president Bashar al-Assad trained to be an eye doctor at St Mary's Hospital in Paddington, London, and his wife Asma was born in Britain to a Syrian family. Told through rarely seen footage and testimony of those who knew and worked with them, the series explores how the couple, who at first were seen as a modernising force, ended up running a regime accused of war crimes

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Charlotte Smith present the show from the Cumbrian Hills, and catch up with a farming mother and son who are diversifying to make ends meet. Charlotte also reports on efforts to protect the surrounding landscape. Plus, Joe Crowley investigates a worrying rise in attacks on livestock by dogs off the leash

programme.title: The Sheriffs Are Coming
programme.desc: The agents attempt to round up six horses that have been abandoned on an unsuspecting family's field. Ben and Myles meet another Sheriff as they arrive at a carpet showroom

programme.title: Frontline Fightback
programme.desc: Rav Wilding reveals how a team of 'super recognisers' - police officers with an extraordinary ability to recognise faces - could be the key to catching criminals who commit multiple offences

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bill Oddie's Top 10 Birds
programme.desc: In the second of three programmes taking a light-hearted look at British wildlife, the naturalist debunks myths and urban legends surrounding Britain's most unpopular birds, showing how their reputations are ill deserved. He also explains how the best-loved species could be seen as villains of the avian world

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Tessa Dunlop hitches a ride with the Thames river police to the new London Gateway, where she gets an overview of Britain's trade with the world from the vantage point of a giant crane. From Ramsgate, Kent, she embarks on one of the Dunkirk 'little ships' to discover the vital role it played during the Second World War, before making her way to Dover where she finds a magnificent Roman lighthouse that has guarded the shores for centuries. Plus, Neil Oliver reveals the stories of trade and defence that characterise England's south east coast

programme.title: Beck
programme.desc: Martin Beck and the team investigate a drugs gang after the death of a young man, putting them on a collision course with an undercover operation. In Swedish

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: In July 1955, Ruth Ellis was the last woman to be hanged in Britain for the cold-blooded murder of lover David Blakely. Gillian Pachter takes a forensic look at the police investigation, and soon discovers worrying assumptions and problematic omissions

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund and Strange head to Afghanistan in search of the killer, while back in Copenhagen Brix conducts a search of the army barracks. Buch becomes certain he has stumbled across a cover-up at the highest levels of the Danish government, but his search for the truth causes turmoil in the cabinet. Elsewhere, Raben faces pressure from all sides to retract his statement. Detective drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing II
programme.desc: Lund and Strange return from Afghanistan with seemingly undeniable evidence, but Brix and his team are trying to come to terms with important new discoveries about the case. Meanwhile, Raben and Jarnvig are forced to work together to keep Louise out of danger, and rumour spreads of plans for a cabinet reshuffle - just as Buch begins to suspect someone has led him astray. Detective drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: The Danish crime drama returns. With 10 days to go to the election in Denmark, Detective Chief Inspector Sarah Lund prepares to celebrate her 25th year in the police force and looks forward to the prospect of a new job in the service. But her relative peace is shattered when body parts are found at Copenhagen dock only hours before a scheduled visit by the prime minister. Starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: The Killing III
programme.desc: Following the kidnapping of Robert Zeuthen's daughter Emilie, Copenhagen Police put their every effort into tracking down the perpetrator and finding the little girl. Meanwhile, Prime Minister Kamper must deal with the fallout of it all. Drama, starring Sofie Grabol. In Danish

programme.title: Nicola Benedetti: BBC Young Musician Winner 2004
programme.desc: Another chance to see the BBC Young Musician of the Year 2004, violinist Nicola Benedetti, performing her triumphant semi-final programme at the Royal Scottish Academy of Music and Drama. Then aged 16, she plays works by Saint-Saens, Janacek and Judith Bingham

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces a performance by the BBC Concert Orchestra of the full score to David Lean's 1957 film. She talks to film composer Debbie Wiseman about what makes it so special, both as a piece in its own right and as an element of the film, and discovers the story of the composer's troubled life, from his committed pacifism during the Second World War to his later struggles with mental illness and alcoholism

programme.title: Maps: Power, Plunder and Possession
programme.desc: Professor Jerry Brotton explains how maps encouraged the plunder and conquest of far-off lands as fabled riches drove explorers such as Christopher Columbus and Walter Raleigh to the New World. He examines the founding of America and the myth of Eldorado, before discussing how cartography made international enterprise possible through the Dutch East India Company and similar ventures

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees investigates the personal animosity between Churchill and Stalin, its basis in more than just political differences, and the threat it posed to their first meeting in August 1942. Having already been betrayed by Hitler, the Soviet leader had a deep mistrust of his new allies, but still went to extraordinary lengths to hide war crimes from them - as new evidence and testimony shows

programme.title: Britain's Most Fragile Treasure
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez explores the complex history of York Minster's Great East Window, the largest medieval stained-glass window in England. Composed of 311 panels, it was originally designed to tell the complete biblical history of the world from creation to the Last Judgement, and has been compared to Michelangelo's work at the Sistine Chapel. The historian closely examines the handiwork of its creator John Thornton, revealing intricate details that would not normally be visible, and follows a project to restore the window to its original glory

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A sensitive and moving account of a year in the life of Sasha, a seven-year-old French girl assigned male at birth, and her family's fight for her acceptance. The film follows her parents as they come to terms with their daughter's gender dysmorphia and reveals how Sasha is affected by the societal norms that make it far from easy for her to experience childhood in the same way as most of her peers

programme.title: Discovery Concerts
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces a performance by the BBC Concert Orchestra of the full score to David Lean's 1957 film. She talks to film composer Debbie Wiseman about what makes it so special, both as a piece in its own right and as an element of the film, and discovers the story of the composer's troubled life, from his committed pacifism during the Second World War to his later struggles with mental illness and alcoholism

programme.title: World War Two: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Laurence Rees investigates the personal animosity between Churchill and Stalin, its basis in more than just political differences, and the threat it posed to their first meeting in August 1942. Having already been betrayed by Hitler, the Soviet leader had a deep mistrust of his new allies, but still went to extraordinary lengths to hide war crimes from them - as new evidence and testimony shows

programme.title: Hemingway
programme.desc: An intimate portrait of Ernest Hemingway, considered one of the greatest American writers. The documentary examines Hemingway's enduring influence on literature and culture and also penetrates the myths surrounding him to reveal a deeply troubled, controversial and ultimately tragic figure. The story begins with his childhood in Illinois, his time with the Red Cross during the First World War, his marriage and his move to Paris, where he started work as a writer

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross demonstrates how to paint a night scene, with waves crashing against rocks and a lighthouse

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Jack and Michelle have an encounter with a school of Mexican dolphins, while casino workers Dale and Mary swap the roulette wheel for rollerblades, sending everyone running for cover

programme.title: Art of Persia
programme.desc: Broadcaster and journalist Samira Ahmed reveals how Iran preserved its distinctive language and culture despite the Arab conquest of Persia

programme.title: Turner: The Man Who Painted Britain
programme.desc: Tim Marlow presents this documentary exploring the achievement of JMW Turner, revealing how the artist's revolutionary works changed the course of painting forever. The programme chronicles the highs and lows of Turner's life, including his tragic childhood, secret affairs and the exhausting extremes to which he pushed himself in order to capture a unique quality in his paintings

programme.title: Simon Schama's Power of Art
programme.desc: Turner's painting The Slave Ship, which courted controversy in its portrayal of slave traders throwing their dying cargo into the sea to claim the insurance money. The work demonstrates the artist's move from traditional styles to a determination to test the limits of both technique and acceptable subject matter

programme.title: What Do Artists Do All Day?
programme.desc: Internationally renowned abstract artist Frank Bowling became the first black Royal Academician in 2005. As a major retrospective opens at Tate Britain, Brenda Emmanus meets him to look back over his long career and there are interviews with critics and fellow artists to discuss the significance of his work in the history of British art

programme.title: Apples, Pears and Paint: How to Make a Still Life Painting
programme.desc: Examining the history of the art form that depicts fruit, flowers and domestic objects, from the Xenia murals at Pompeii through to the cubist works of Picasso. The film also takes a look at the work of some of the artists synonymous with the genre, including Chardin and Cezanne, and features contributions from Bettany Hughes, Janina Ramirez and Andrew Graham-Dixon

programme.title: The Joy of Painting

programme.title: Turner: The Man Who Painted Britain
programme.desc: Tim Marlow presents this documentary exploring the achievement of JMW Turner, revealing how the artist's revolutionary works changed the course of painting forever. The programme chronicles the highs and lows of Turner's life, including his tragic childhood, secret affairs and the exhausting extremes to which he pushed himself in order to capture a unique quality in his paintings

Or maybe it’s Sid the Snail practicing his meditation? Betty needs to find out if she’s ever to get her little ones to sleep.

programme.title: My Very First
programme.desc: Join 10-month-old Teddy and Ada as they discover the world around them and learn to communicate through signs and words. Ada and Teddy are both at home playing with toy phones in the garden. Teddy’s Mum takes him to a BSL class where they play with bubbles to help them learn some signs. Ada goes to a signing class and learns the signs for “more” and “milk”. Teddy then visits a butterfly house and gets to see some reptiles and meerkats! After lunch, Teddy’s siblings show him how to use a shape sorting toy, and Ada visits the park to play a game of find the tennis ball. Finally, Teddy practices the signs he learned with his family. Remember, every child develops differently.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome little ones Oakley, Arthur, Luna, Reau, Ty’lana and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Parents, carers and their babies at home are invited to join in with all the fun activities too! Inside Giovanna’s bag is a toy camera, and all the parents and babies have different objects and toys that are themed around a day out. These are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby-appropriate toys and objects inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song Looking Out on Our Day Out features, as does a story involving Baby Bear. Viewers at home show how they’ve been exploring things that are themed around a day out too. The Together Song gets everyone moving about, before some calm time with sensory bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

Everley visits her family dentist, where the dentist checks her teeth and talks to mum about the importance of teething. Once home, Everley’s mum gives Everley her very own toothbrush! programme.title: Maddie, the Plants and You

Find out how flowers and different animals, like bees, birds, butterflies and insects, work together. We learn how bees and insects pollinate flowers, as Maddie becomes a bee and plays the pollination game! Along the way, we’ll discover flowers of all different shapes, sizes and colours and learn why they are so different.
