programme.title: Colours
programme.desc: Series for young children teaching them about colour. In this episode, we learn about objects that are associated with the colour gold, from treasure to golden sand, and explore how gold metal is used. The episode concludes with a specially written poem summarising the colour gold.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin goes ice skating with his friends and Mr Tumble builds a cosy den in his bedroom

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: For party time the Teletubbies have balloons, party blowers and lots of sparkles as they set off to visit the Tiddlytubbies and do a fun party dance. In Tummy Tales, twins celebrate their birthday party with all of their friends

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny makes his own magic stone when Sula won't involve him in her game - but disaster strikes

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: The four adventure-seeking superheroes must try to stop some runaway Chinese drums as they roll towards the Forbidden City

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: Finlay and Timmy have fun playing scary Halloween tricks on their friends - until they hear spooky noises coming from the outhouse

programme.title: Footy Pups
programme.desc: Rachel Yankey teaches today's team all about overhead throwing. At Pupton Park, there is a new teacher in town in the form of Professor Pickles

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Grace shows off three amazing machines, all of which are different kinds of motorbikes

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain ignores Gem's warning that blowing her whistle too much could 'bring bad luck on the wind'

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are helping Duggee in the garden when Tag makes friends with a caterpillar. She is very hungry, so Tag feeds her the vegetable leaves they have been picking

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When the rabbits find themselves surrounded, Peter is forced to create a brand new ingenious escape route

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Ruby sings a diva-style ballad about the thing she loves more than anything else - school

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: CBeebies presenter Andy is jetting off all over the world and meeting amazing animals living in and nearby water. Get ready to explore oceans, rivers and lakes

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Doctor Bluey is needed when Dad gets a very curious x-ray from Nurse Bingo. It seems he has a cat in his tummy, leaving Bluey no choice but to operate immediately!

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Grandmaster Glitch's digging floods Wieliczka Saltmine. The Go Jetters must save the famous salt statues

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Molly examines her shelf of special things at the back of Mack’s Stall. She chooses the model space rocket and remembers the day she got it. Caitlin’s favourite dungarees, complete with space rocket patches, are getting too small for her. She doesn’t want to admit it, even though they make her wriggle about. Moira thinks she needs to get a new pair. Meanwhile, Daisy spots some beautiful earrings on Alice’s Stall. Alice tells her she got them on her travels in South America. Caitlin is still fidgeting in her tight dungarees but she tries not to let Granny Moira see. Later, at the Kids Club, Caitlin is making a model spaceship, and she tells James she wants to be an astronaut when she grows up. Daisy decides to buy the earrings and thinks it could be fun to learn to samba dance. This gives Alice an idea! Alice tells James she’s going to teach the children samba dancing the next day. Caitlin is keen to have a little practice in advance. All goes well, until Caitlin makes a big move and ‘p

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Gram and Grand visit. Waffle loves Gram; he'll only leave his new dog doormat for her

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo tries to grow a daffodil, but is disheartened to discover that it will take a long time to bloom. Cynthia shows JoJo how to take care of the plant, and Gran Gran shows JoJo a way to measure how much it is growing.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah's new colour wheel reminds Rainbow that he has not seen his niece in a while

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: When magical toy Pepi Nana wakes up in the night, she writes a letter to the moon

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy dances in the garden with the Haahoos and the daisies

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: You're all invited to a party with Tom Hardy in tonight's bedtime story but there is one rule... you must bring a hat!

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jane Baxter and Philip Juma. Helen McGinn joins via video link and is picking the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Our Food, Our Family with Michela Chiappa
programme.desc: Born in 1962 to Nigerian parents studying medicine in London, Kemi Nevins now runs a successful cafe in Abercynon serving up delicious food. However, none of that she offers reflects her Nigerian background. Now Kemi's children are adults themselves, they would like to know more about their heritage, so the Welsh-Italian cook visits Kemi to help her reconnect to her roots through her love of food

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day six of the tournament at the All England Club, where third-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played. Top seed Ashleigh Barty had too much for British wildcard Harriet Dart at this stage when the tournament was last played in 2019, prevailing 6-1, 6-1. Another British player to suffer disappointment was Dan Evans, who lost in five sets to Joao Sousa, while Andy Murray experienced mixed fortunes, winning alongside Serena Williams in the mixed doubles after he and Pierre-Hugues Herbert had gone out of the men's doubles

programme.title: MOTD Live: UEFA Euro 2020
programme.desc: Ukraine v England (Kick-off 8.00pm). Coverage of the concluding quarter-final, held at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, as the winners of the seventh and eighth last-16 matches battle it out. Gareth Southgate's men finally ended their tournament hoodoo against Germany on Tuesday, scoring twice in the last 15 minutes at Wembley to defeat their old enemy in a knockout game for the first time since the 1966 World Cup Final. Raheem Sterling notched his third goal of the tournament to send the crowd into raptures, and captain Harry Kane finished the job with a neat header from Jack Grealish's cross. Ukraine progressed thanks to a 2-1 victory over Sweden at Hampden Park, with Artem Dovbyk striking in added time in extra time

programme.title: Crouchy's Year Late Euros: Live
programme.desc: Former England striker Peter Crouch gives his unique take on the latest Euro 2020 action alongside Maya Jama, with music from Alex Horne and the Horne Section

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the best moments from the tournament in France

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Euro 2020 Replay
programme.desc: A chance to see one of today's quarter-finals from either Olympic Stadium in Baku or Stadio Olimpico in Rome

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales, presented by James Williams

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a case from 1882 of a wife accused of poisoning her husband's rice pudding with enough arsenic to kill 50 people

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a three bed semi in Kidsgrove, Stoke-on-Trent, a split level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached house in Surrey

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Brenda Edwards explores the magnificent Chatsworth House in Derbyshire, and introduces a feast of Christian music including performances from Lesley Garrett and the Overtones

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, and visits a pottery whose inspired by the Welsh countryside. With Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
programme.desc: A hero of ancient Greece vows to save a princess from being sacrificed to a ferocious sea monster. However, to defeat the creature he must first embark on a journey into the underworld and seize the head of the monstrous Medusa, whose gaze turns men to stone. Mythical adventure, starring Harry Hamlin, Laurence Olivier, Maggie Smith and Judi Bowker, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads into the wilds of Snowdonia with disabled rambler Terry Taylor as they explore an old Roman road leading across the Carneddau Mountains. He then goes south to Skomer Island off Pembrokeshire where, guided by warden Bee Buche, he gets a close-up view of the puffin population before staying up late to see the reclusive Manx shearwater

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen looks at how people get from A to B in Wales, featuring archive clips of motorcycle stunts, runaway buses and giant inflatables

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Pizza chefs Jez and Ieuz continue their adventure across Europe in San Sebastian, where they tour the Spanish city's best tabernas sampling pintxos, before heading to Bordeaux

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison celebrate the 150th anniversary of Wimbledon Common, encountering the horseback patrols that enforce the Common's 57 bylaws and discovering how the Wombles have inspired a whole new generation of ecologists. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to protect the world's oceans are delivering for UK waters, and Dwayne Fields leads four youngsters at a crossroad in their lives on an expedition into the Welsh wilderness

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: Wild Man to Ironman
programme.desc: Mat Pritchard takes on his biggest endurance challenge yet, as he aims to be the first person to swim, cycle, and run around the coastline and border of Wales in just 14 days. The programme also looks back at his party-boy years as a member of Dirty Sanchez, and how he has now transformed his life and turned into a ultra-endurance athlete and became an inspiration to others

programme.title: Molly's Game (2017)
programme.desc: The true story of Molly Bloom, an Olympic-class skier who ran the world's most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested in the middle of the night by FBI agents wielding automatic weapons. Her players included Hollywood royalty, sports stars, business titans and finally - unbeknownst to her - the Russian Mob. Drama directed by The West Wing creator Aaron Sorkin, starring Jessica Chastain, Idris Elba and Kevin Costner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a notorious case of supposed matricide dating from the 1920s, which may have resulted in an innocent man being hanged

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Ross presents the show from Brackley, Northamptonshire, and discovers how old food can be collectable. With experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day seven of the tournament at the All England Club, where fourth-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta delighted the home crowd when she came from a set down to defeat two-time champion Petra Kvitova at this stage two years ago. She was followed on Centre Court by Roger Federer, who was in dominant form as he overwhelmed 17th seed Matteo Berrettini 6-1, 6-2, 6-2

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Iolo's Pembrokeshire
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores Pembrokeshire's stunning landscape and goes in search of its hidden wildlife spots, finding a rare butterfly and an old oil storage site where hundreds of orchids have bloomed. Iolo also walks round St David's Head, where he finds fearsome looking beetles and Britain's only venomous snake

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
programme.desc: A hero of ancient Greece vows to save a princess from being sacrificed to a ferocious sea monster. programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond visits the 17th-century harbour of Portsoy in Aberdeenshire, to find out how the Scottish Traditional Boat Festival has gone online and takes a trip on a 1960s fishing boat with a colourful history

programme.title: Idris Elba Meets Paul McCartney
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, two of the biggest names in entertainment come together for a very special one-off show, as the music icon is interviewed by the Golden Globe-winning actor. Idris finds out what inspires Paul to continue to innovate creatively, on the eve of the release of his 26th post-Beatles album McCartney III, featuring Paul playing every instrument and writing and recording every song

programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy makers on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and the Rev Kate Bottley present topical debate covering religious, moral and ethical issues, with guest panellists and contributions from viewers

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a case from 1882 of a wife accused of poisoning her husband's rice pudding with enough arsenic to kill 50 people

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell explore a three bed semi in Kidsgrove, Stoke-on-Trent, a split level maisonette in Brixton, and a detached house in Surrey

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Brenda Edwards explores the magnificent Chatsworth House in Derbyshire, and introduces a feast of Christian music including performances from Lesley Garrett and the Overtones

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents the show from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, and visits a pottery whose inspired by the Welsh countryside. With Philip Serrell and Thomas Plant

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a high-energy house hunt in the Norfolk countryside where he helps a dynamic couple quit urban life in London and find a character family home for their £400,000 budget

programme.title: Clash of the Titans (1981)
programme.desc: A hero of ancient Greece vows to save a princess from being sacrificed to a ferocious sea monster. However, to defeat the creature he must first embark on a journey into the underworld and seize the head of the monstrous Medusa, whose gaze turns men to stone. Mythical adventure, starring Harry Hamlin, Laurence Olivier, Maggie Smith and Judi Bowker, with special effects by Ray Harryhausen

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: Natural history documentary in which spy cameras camouflaged as various creatures go 'undercover' in the animal world, getting up close and personal to film their behaviour. This time, cameras follow Japanese macaques in the snow-covered mountains of Japan, as they gather at steaming hot thermal pools to bathe and socialise. Elsewhere in the cool mountains of Mexico, cameras capture a spectacular gathering of billions of monarch butterflies, and in Bavaria, Germany, Spy Beaver gains unique access to the secretive world of the beaver

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Ellie Harrison celebrate the 150th anniversary of Wimbledon Common, encountering the horseback patrols that enforce the Common's 57 bylaws and discovering how the Wombles have inspired a whole new generation of ecologists. Tom Heap investigates whether efforts to protect the world's oceans are delivering for UK waters, and Dwayne Fields leads four youngsters at a crossroad in their lives on an expedition into the Welsh wilderness

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from the National Botanic Garden of Wales in Carmarthenshire, exploring the Victorian mania for collecting exotic plants and examining rare species grown inside the gigantic bio-dome. The team assesses items brought in by the public, with Duncan Campbell inspecting a Chinese box that Hollywood star Gregory Peck tried - and failed - to buy, and John Axford is amazed to see an extremely rare 12th-century bronze statuette brought back from Calcutta in the 1920s

programme.title: Yesterday (2019)
programme.desc: Jack, a struggling musician, gets hit by a bus during a mysterious event. When he regains consciousness, he finds that he is the only person who can remember the Beatles, and sets out to exploit the opportunity by passing their hits off as his own compositions. Danny Boyle's comedy, starring Himesh Patel and Lily James

programme.title: The Irish Open - Highlights
programme.desc: Stephen Watson presents action from day four at Mount Juliet Estate in Thomastown, Kilkenny, as the tournament won by America's John Catlin in 2020 concluded. The American secured the title in style in Ballymena, carding a six-under-par round of 64 to finish two strokes clear of England's Aaron Rai. With analysis from Des Smyth

programme.title: Match of the Day Top 10
programme.desc: Gary Lineker is joined by Alan Shearer and Micah Richards to count down the best moments from the tournament in France

programme.title: Molly's Game (2017)
programme.desc: The true story of Molly Bloom, an Olympic-class skier who ran the world's most exclusive high-stakes poker game for a decade before being arrested in the middle of the night by FBI agents wielding automatic weapons. Her players included Hollywood royalty, sports stars, business titans and finally - unbeknownst to her - the Russian Mob. Drama directed by The West Wing creator Aaron Sorkin, starring Jessica Chastain, Idris Elba and Kevin Costner

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin catches up with an ambitious project to create a forever home in Crewe. Plus, a former stables in Derby and a studio flat in London's Paddington

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine a notorious case of supposed matricide dating from the 1920s, which may have resulted in an innocent man being hanged

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, following the work of those providing emergency and medical care to the Shetland Islands' 23,000 residents

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Ross presents the show from Brackley, Northamptonshire, and discovers how old food can be collectable. With experts Thomas Forrester and Stephanie Connell

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Wimbledon 2021
programme.desc: Sue Barker presents live coverage on day seven of the tournament at the All England Club, where fourth-round matches in the men's and women's singles are scheduled to be played. British number one Johanna Konta delighted the home crowd when she came from a set down to defeat two-time champion Petra Kvitova at this stage two years ago. She was followed on Centre Court by Roger Federer, who was in dominant form as he overwhelmed 17th seed Matteo Berrettini 6-1, 6-2, 6-2

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: The first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat

programme.title: Life Is Magic
programme.desc: Illusionist Joel M from Bangor, Co Down, takes his conjuring skills to parties and events around the country, helping to create life-changing celebrations that people will talk about for years to come. In the first edition he meets the student formal committee at Our Lady of Lourdes High School in Ballymoney to help plan an epic trick for their formal dance

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Frankie is feeling downbeat so Zack offers to take her on a driving lesson - but his good deed has terrible consequences. Kat asks Denise to reconsider letting Phil into Raymond's life, Patrick returns the things that Isaac stole to a shocked Ruby and Martin, and Peter resolves to help a heartbroken Bobby move on with his life

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Report on British businesses dealing with post-Brexit trading rules six months after the UK left the EU single market, trying to maintain relationships with their European customers while also seeking new markets further afield. The programme features personal accounts from fishermen in Scotland to entrepreneurs in the home counties

programme.title: Gold Rush: Our Race to Olympic Glory
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how Britain turned its sporting reputation around over 16 years to triumph at the 2012 Olympics. The first episode begins with the UK's poor performance in the 1996 games at Atlanta, and how the Government diverted National Lottery funding to provide the best possible training for the nation's athletes. Featuring contributions from Linford Christie and Kelly Holmes

programme.title: Countryside College
programme.desc: The summer term sees the nation in lockdown due to the pandemic. The CAFRE students continue their studies online and many have become essential workers. Final year agricultural student Jack Nevin hoped to graduate in June, but his college days have been cut short and he finds himself back working on the family dairy farm outside Portrush

programme.title: High: Confessions of an Ibiza Drug Mule
programme.desc: The case of Michaella McCollum, a teenager from Northern Ireland who formed a relationship with a drug trafficker during her first trip abroad in Ibiza. He persuaded her to assist him in smuggling drugs for his cartel contacts, and just a few weeks later, she was arrested at Lima airport in Peru with more than a million pounds worth of cocaine in her suitcase

programme.title: Cristiano Ronaldo: Impossible to Ignore
programme.desc: The story of Portuguese football star Cristiano Ronaldo's life and career, revealing how he became the world's biggest sporting brand

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Actor Stephen Mangan hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with comedian Janey Godley and journalist Helen Lewis joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.desc: Recorded coverage of House of Commons proceedings, from Tuesday 29 June

programme.title: Gudrun - A' Bhana-phrionnsa Lochlannach
programme.desc: The autumn chill is turning the forest gold when King Sigurd takes Gudrun to gather nuts for a feast. However, after Gudrun falls asleep beside a tree she awakes to find her basket empty. She's not the only one gathering nuts for the winter

programme.title: Treubh an Tuathanais
programme.desc: When Coinneach the cockerel decides that he is the best person to be the farm leader, Mona challenges him and, as a result of this, they put it to a vote

programme.title: Òrain CBeebies
programme.desc: A selection of seafaring songs from Carrie, Linda, Ruairidh and the CBeebies ALBA presenters

programme.title: 'S E Iasg a Th'Annam (I'm a Fish)
programme.desc: The Moonfish tells us that they are the only fish who have a warm heart and that their bodies are warmer than the waters around them when they swim to the depths of the ocean

programme.title: Nannag a' Noo/Huggleboo
programme.desc: Huggleboo?discovers little seeds in an apple. From each seed a plant can grow, and each plant can turn into a tree

programme.title: Padraig Post: Seirbheis Deis agus Sonraichte

Plus, three races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Lancashire Oaks and 3.15 Old Newton Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reporting from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman at Sandown and Sally Ann Grassick from Haydock, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the third quarter-final, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. After only finishing third in their group below England and Croatia, the Czechs produced one of the shocks of the tournament so far in their last-16 tie with the Netherlands, capitalising on Matthijs de Ligt's red card to secure a 2-0 victory courtesy of strikes by Tomas Holes and Patrik Schick. However, they now face another tough task against the Danes, who were shaken by talisman Christian Eriksen's cardiac arrest and lost their opening two matches to Iceland and Belgium, but have since gone from strength to strength, defeating Russia 4-1, before overwhelming Wales 4-0 in the last 16, as they look to emulate their triumph in Euro 92. Commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Ally McCoist

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights

Alan plans to give her a peaceful retreat outside her back door with flavours of Africa and the Caribbean, as well as more than a splash of purple, her favourite colour. He also reflects Joan's community spirit by challenging the team to make over three front gardens, finding different ways of revitalising these tired and forgotten spaces

The ninja who makes it furthest in this year's final, should no-one reach the top of Mt Midoriyama, will pocket $100,000, and the prize for scaling the mountain is a cool $300,000

The programme meets the family as the twins approach their fourth birthday, and their parents face the agonising decision of whether to put them through complex surgery to separate them. To help them make this fateful decision, the family heads on an emotional journey to meet other conjoined twins and their parents. Narrated by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures

The programme also presents behind the scenes footage from Joanna's travels in Haiti and Cuba, including previously unseen footage of her other adventures in the Caribbean

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef pays culinary homage to his mother's cooking by preparing some childhood favourites, including vegetable soup three ways and an assiette of salads made with produce fresh from his gardens. He is joined by Nathan Outlaw, who serves up a dish of fish and chips with mushy peas

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.35 Coral-Eclipse from Sandown Park, which follows races at 1.50 and 3.00. Plus, three races from Haydock Park, including the 2.40 Lancashire Oaks and 3.15 Old Newton Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reporting from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman at Sandown and Sally Ann Grassick from Haydock, and commentary by Richard Hoiles

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Live
programme.desc: Czech Republic v Denmark (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the third quarter-final, which takes place at Olympic Stadium in Baku, Azerbaijan. After only finishing third in their group below England and Croatia, the Czechs produced one of the shocks of the tournament so far in their last-16 tie with the Netherlands, capitalising on Matthijs de Ligt's red card to secure a 2-0 victory courtesy of strikes by Tomas Holes and Patrik Schick. However, they now face another tough task against the Danes, who were shaken by talisman Christian Eriksen's cardiac arrest and lost their opening two matches to Iceland and Belgium, but have since gone from strength to strength, defeating Russia 4-1, before overwhelming Wales 4-0 in the last 16, as they look to emulate their triumph in Euro 92. Commentary by Clive Tyldesley and Ally McCoist

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Perry Fenwick, Jenny Eclair, Ore Oduba and Richard Madeley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Pitch Perfect (2012)
programme.desc: Under heavy pressure from her father, Beca reluctantly starts her first year at Barden University and gets an internship at the school radio station. She soon acquires a new circle of friends in the form of a band of misfits with a shared talent for singing, joiining their a cappella group and and trying to inject some much needed energy into their repertoire. Comedy, starring Anna Kendrick, Brittany Snow, Anna Camp, Rebel Wilson and Skylar Astin

programme.title: UEFA Euro 2020 Highlights
programme.desc: Mark Pougatch presents action from today's quarter-finals, which were Ukraine v England at Stadio Olimpico in Rome, and Czech Republic v Denmark at Olympic Stadium in Baku. Commentators include Sam Matterface, Lee Dixon, Clive Tyldesley, and Ally McCoist

programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team are in London, where they transform the run-down garden of Joan Myers, a senior paediatric nurse who has worked tirelessly to help improve the way children are cared for within the NHS - but in doing so, has let her own garden become overgrown. Alan plans to give her a peaceful retreat outside her back door with flavours of Africa and the Caribbean, as well as more than a splash of purple, her favourite colour. programme.desc: Rebecca Maddern, Ben Fordham and Freddie Flintoff present the Grand Final of the show in which contestants take on a formidable assault course in Melbourne. The best 30 ninjas have qualified, and now have nine obstacles to overcome against the clock. If they can conquer it in three minutes and 15 seconds, they'll go onto stage two and three. The ninja who makes it furthest in this year's final, should no-one reach the top of Mt Midoriyama, will pocket $100,000, and the prize for scaling the mountain is a cool $300,000

However key royal insider reveal all, including the details of significant private fallings-out between them that occurred long before their issues became public

programme.title: Extraordinary Twins
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary following Nick and Chelsea from Idaho, whose twin daughters Callie and Carter are joined together at the sternum. The programme meets the family as the twins approach their fourth birthday, and their parents face the agonising decision of whether to put them through complex surgery to separate them. To help them make this fateful decision, the family heads on an emotional journey to meet other conjoined twins and their parents. Narrated by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's Unseen Adventures
programme.desc: The actress presents a final selection of previously unseen footage from her travelogue programmes, revealing unseen stories and special moments of how these journeys are made. This one follows her adventures in and around Iran, from where she followed the Silk Road through Azerbaijan and Georgia, finally moving on to Venice. The programme also presents behind the scenes footage from Joanna's travels in Haiti and Cuba, including previously unseen footage of her other adventures in the Caribbean

Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: Step Up (2006)
programme.desc: An aspiring ballerina finds her life turned upside down when she encounters a troubled street-smart teenager from the wrong side of town. But as the pair get together to work on a dance project, they develop a mutual admiration, and love begins to blossom between them. Romantic drama, starring Channing Tatum and Jenna Dewan

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: The latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Step Up (2006)

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: Relationship-based reality show in which singletons seeking a romance compete in the hope of finding love - and winning a cash prize. Presented by Laura Whitmore

programme.title: Love Island: Aftersun
programme.desc: Laura Whitmore is joined by a panel of celebrity superfans to debrief all the latest news and gossip from the nation's favourite Villa

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Lois send Chris to space camp to boost his self-esteem. When his stint ends and the Griffins arrive to pick him up, they are accidentally launched into space and it is down to the awkward youth to land the clan safely back on Earth

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Lois causes Peter some distress when she tells him about her dinner date with former boyfriend Ross Fishman, his wife Pam, and their son Ben. Ross brags about their family vacations and that they plan to climb Mount Everest, so Peter decides the Griffins will also make the undertaking. When they return home, he realises his folly and asks Lois to make up an excuse to bail out

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: When Steve swears at Francine, she refuses to cook for him and he becomes a successful chef, appearing on a popular morning show. Meanwhile, Stan thinks he can make people invisible by snapping his fingers

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan and his CIA colleagues set off on a mission to Thailand to blow up a weapons cache, not realising Bullock has already sent a drone to do the job. Meanwhile, Francine and the rest of the family take part in a slow-motion race against each other in the living room

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland and friends visit Goochland for the annual football match against their high school rivals, but an accident leaves them scrambling to find an escape from their enemies' territory. Meanwhile, Donna's provocative Halloween costume gets her into trouble with the police, and Roberta and Cleveland Jr play tricks dressed as their parents

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna insists the family helps at the homeless shelter at Christmas, but Cleveland is surprised to find the food there is better than what he eats at home. Meanwhile, Roberta is hired as an elf at a department store, but her chauvinistic employer requires her and other female employees to wear skimpy outfits

programme.title: Shopping with Keith Lemon
programme.desc: Keith Lemon is back to take more famous faces out shopping and other fun non-shopping related activities whilst having a chat about their life and career. Prepare for gossip, laughs and shocking revelations. Here, Keith takes the one and only Gemma Collins for afternoon tea on the Thames and chat show legend Jonathan Ross shows Keith his toy collection.

programme.title: You've Been Framed!

programme.desc: n London, model Elesha is hoping to be impressed by the style choices of three potential suitors. At the end of the challenge, which of the three competitors will she choose to take out on a date?

programme.title: Secret Crush

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Jonah and Amy travel to her childhood home to help her parents move out. Glenn and Garrett vie for the Cloud 9 Integrity Award, while Dina uses Mateo's relationship with Jeff to fix problems within the store. America Ferrera stars

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Mateo's undocumented status causes trouble once again, while Glenn and Amy do battle with an online troll. Jonah and Cheyenne provide legal counsel for Mateo - though it could prove unhelpful. Comedy, starring America Ferrera

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: After hearing that Boo Boo is leaving Boyz 4 Now, Louise enters a contest that could finally give Tina a chance to meet him. Meanwhile, Bob and Linda learn about a mobster

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina finally gets to attend horse camp, but realises she must part ways with her imaginary steed Jericho. Meanwhile, Linda creates a restaurant camp

programme.title: Love Island

programme.title: Celebrity Karaoke Club
programme.desc: The doors to the Celebrity Karaoke Club are open again and there's another all-star guest list. Tonight Olivia Attwood, Brian Dowling, AJ Odudu, Sonny Jay, Tanya Bardsley, Max and Harvey and Amelia Lily hit the karaoke stage and the free bar

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian writes a play that becomes a hit in Quahog, but loses his confidence when he finds that Stewie is also a budding playwright and is better at it. When the toddler sees how upset his friend is, he decides to make amends. Animation, featuring the guest voice of playwright Alan Bennett as himself

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins receive a letter from the Nielsen Company telling them they have been selected to have their television viewing habits monitored. Tom Tucker comes to the door and asks Peter for some ideas to change the format of the news show, and the ludicrous suggestions include the anchorman shaving off his moustache and wearing a jester hat

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A broke Hayley gets an office job and falls for a cute colleague, prompting her to consider embarking on an affair while on a work retreat to Belize. Meanwhile, Stan chauffeurs Steve and Francine to a mother-son dance, but things turn ugly when he refuses to let them dance together

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve does his best to stop Snot becoming a bully after his friend realises he is a natural at karate. Meanwhile, Klaus develops a horrible odour and is forced to become an outdoor pet

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: An outbreak of headlice at school forces Rallo to cut off his beloved Afro, and when the other kids make fun of him, Cleveland Jr steps in to look after his little brother. Director Patty Jenkins' blockbusting fantasy adventure, starring Gal Gadot, Chris Pine and David Thewlis

This time, a drone is destroyed by an errant baseball, a seal refuses to join in on a selfie, and a falling tree traps what seems to be a familiar face in the toilet

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a special celebrity edition of the classic game show, with contestants Lesley Garrett, Jools Holland and Adil Ray all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Jonah and Amy travel to her childhood home to help her parents move out. Glenn and Garrett vie for the Cloud 9 Integrity Award, while Dina uses Mateo's relationship with Jeff to fix problems within the store. America Ferrera stars

programme.title: Superstore
programme.desc: Mateo's undocumented status causes trouble once again, while Glenn and Amy do battle with an online troll. Jonah and Cheyenne provide legal counsel for Mateo - though it could prove unhelpful. Comedy, starring America Ferrera

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: After hearing that Boo Boo is leaving Boyz 4 Now, Louise enters a contest that could finally give Tina a chance to meet him. Meanwhile, Bob and Linda learn about a mobster

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina finally gets to attend horse camp, but realises she must part ways with her imaginary steed Jericho. Meanwhile, Linda creates a restaurant camp

programme.title: Love Island
programme.desc: The relationship-based reality show continues with the single people living like celebrities in the villa, all under the watchful gaze of viewers. Laura Whitmore presents

programme.title: Celebrity Karaoke Club
programme.desc: The doors to the Celebrity Karaoke Club are open again and there's another all-star guest list. Tonight Olivia Attwood, Brian Dowling, AJ Odudu, Sonny Jay, Tanya Bardsley, Max and Harvey and Amelia Lily hit the karaoke stage and the free bar

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Brian writes a play that becomes a hit in Quahog, but loses his confidence when he finds that Stewie is also a budding playwright and is better at it. When the toddler sees how upset his friend is, he decides to make amends. Animation, featuring the guest voice of playwright Alan Bennett as himself

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins receive a letter from the Nielsen Company telling them they have been selected to have their television viewing habits monitored. Tom Tucker comes to the door and asks Peter for some ideas to change the format of the news show, and the ludicrous suggestions include the anchorman shaving off his moustache and wearing a jester hat

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: A broke Hayley gets an office job and falls for a cute colleague, prompting her to consider embarking on an affair while on a work retreat to Belize. Meanwhile, Stan chauffeurs Steve and Francine to a mother-son dance, but things turn ugly when he refuses to let them dance together

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Steve does his best to stop Snot becoming a bully after his friend realises he is a natural at karate. Meanwhile, Klaus develops a horrible odour and is forced to become an outdoor pet

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: An outbreak of headlice at school forces Rallo to cut off his beloved Afro, and when the other kids make fun of him, Cleveland Jr steps in to look after his little brother. Meanwhile, Freight Train and Cookie leave Cleveland to house-sit for the weekend, but things go badly wrong when his friends visit

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Donna is annoyed when Cleveland and Cookie spend time with members of the Silver Sunset Society after they are mistaken for husband and wife. Meanwhile, Rallo steals chocolate from a street vendor, but tries to put the blame on Cleveland Junior when he is caught

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: After hearing that Boo Boo is leaving Boyz 4 Now, Louise enters a contest that could finally give Tina a chance to meet him. Meanwhile, Bob and Linda learn about a mobster

programme.title: Bob's Burgers
programme.desc: Tina finally gets to attend horse camp, but realises she must part ways with her imaginary steed Jericho. Meanwhile, Linda creates a restaurant camp

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmondson narrates more record feats, including the world's longest tongue and the fastest escape from a straitjacket underwater

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: Roger's captaincy hangs in the balance as the votes are cast at the EGM, but Shirley provides an unexpected diversion. Cricketing comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Outside Edge
programme.desc: As Brent Park Cricket Club prepare for the opening ceremony of their new pitch and pavilion, Mim arranges a big surprise for Rog - but disaster is waiting in the wings. Comedy drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Robert Daws, Timothy Spall and Josie Lawrence

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alina's baby bombshell throws Tyrone into turmoil. Jenny puts Daisy firmly in her place. James asks Danny to revive their relationship

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: David pesters Gail to allow Nick to see Sam. James attracts unwanted online attention prior to a press conference. Fiz offers to support Tyrone

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Family feuding causes heartache for Gail. James bravely goes public with his sexuality. Tyrone avoids telling Fiz of Alina's pregnancy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Carry On Abroad (1972)
programme.desc: British holiday-makers jet off on a package holiday to the Spanish resort of Elsbels. Unluckily, not only is the hotel short of staff, but it also has no walls, ceilings or entertainment. Further mishaps leave the tourists fuming - until they sample a local punch laced with an aphrodisiac. Comedy, with Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Charles Hawtrey and Joan Sims

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Nelson discover that the exciting world of stunt pilots and military heroes hides many dark secrets after the owner of Finchmere Airfield is hit on the head and dropped to his death from a plane. Are anti-aircraft campaigners behind Bernard King's murder, or is the incident linked to a tragic Second World War mission? Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee are joined by guest stars including June Whitfield, Robert Bathurst, Bernard Cribbins and Laila Rouass

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby's secret past as a spy is revealed when a former agent is killed after a cricket match between the villagers and the gentry. The dead man's wounds appear to have been inflicted by a giant claw, triggering rumours of a 'beast of Midsomer', and before long, another body turns up with similar injuries. Barnaby then realises the incidents are linked to the time the local network of spies spent in Cold War Berlin. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Anna Massey, Peter Davison and Neil Stuke

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective investigates a suspected kidnapping and gets his first lead when a ransom note is discovered. The missing woman then turns up dead at the wheel of a crashed car, smelling of alcohol. Whodunit, starring Jack Shepherd, Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow George Jeffers threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, which he believes is potentially lethal, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. Detective drama, guest starring David Haig and Nigel Whitmey, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A masterpiece by landscape artist Henry Hogson causes a stir when it is auctioned for £400,000 after a fierce bidding war - and hours later, its former owner is tortured and killed. When a Hogson work is then stolen, Barnaby and Jones research the Midsomer painter with the help of art teacher Matilda Simms. They soon uncover evidence of forgery - but the killer is still at large. Guest starring David Bamber and Susannah Harker, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An aristocrat's wealthy, overbearing wife finances a North African archaeological dig to find the skull of John the Baptist. Poirot is in attendance, but when the woman is found stabbed to death, and only her husband, the exuberant Lord Boynton, appears distraught, the detective struggles to identify the killer among the wealth of suspects. Guest starring Tim Curry, Cheryl Campbell, John Hannah, Emma Cunniffe, Mark Gatiss, Angela Pleasence and Beth Goddard, with David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family members are surprised to find out that Leslie has three different girlfriends, so Louisa decides to take it upon herself to help him pick one by having each member of the family invite their favourite girl to afternoon tea. Larry injures his leg and Theo decides to use his newly set-up x-ray unit to try and examine it. Elsewhere, Gerry continues to grow his animal collection, Margo takes up a job as Theo's assistant, and Aunt Hermione arrives in Corfu for good

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean spies on Sid when his bad timekeeping makes her suspicious of his activities. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Owing to a little white lie on his income tax returns, Mr Roper must quickly acquire a son. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Meena causes trouble. Faith is determined to get Liv back on track. Charles spreads the word about Emmerdale Pride

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The collection tin is missing. Faith is scared. Tracy is struggling

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard spots there is something wrong with Faith. Jacob is suspicious of Meena and confronts her. The Vivienne opens Emmerdale Pride

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith faces her fate at the hospital. Andrea and Charles cannot resist each other. Leanna is upset with her father

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Faith gives the wrong idea. Vic and David bond over their babies. Ben takes a mysterious and unwelcome phone call

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pollard sympathises, but Faith needs to be truthful. Meena begins to plot. Angry Liv sows doubt in Aaron's mind

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: The detective applies his professional know-how to a fatal shooting, and soon begins to suspect that the dead man's brother-in-law may know more about the case than he is letting on - a theory borne out by forensic tests on the murder weapon. Jack Shepherd stars as the dour detective, with Helen Masters and Jimmy Yuill

programme.title: Wycliffe
programme.desc: Two grieving widows lay rival claims to a corpse found washed up on the beach, each insisting it is the body of her missing husband. While it is obvious only one can be telling the truth, the detective finds it tricky to discern which of the women is lying - and why. Drama, guest starring Eleanor Bron, with Jack Shepherd

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe

programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sally feels she cannot cope without the girls. Vera and Jack are furious when Alec docks their rent from their wages. Jim urges Fiona not to cast Steve away over just one mistake

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Natalie helps Kevin out by looking after Sophie. Jim accuses Fiona of lying to him to get back at her for wrecking her wedding. She tells him Steve really did try to kill him

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris is heading for a fall, with neither Turner nor Terry willing to tolerate being ordered around by him. Ashley cancels the pantomime. Butch invites John round for Christmas

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is the day of the Christmas Show, but Ashley has no star act. Zak's latest money-making scheme does not go to plan. Marc disrupts Sean and Angie's plans for a family Christmas

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local gamekeeper is injured in a shooting incident on the moors and suspicion falls on his disgruntled predecessor, whose 11-year-old daughter provides damaging testimony. Elsewhere, Maggie discovers her ex-husband has started work at the hospital and Greengrass schemes away as usual, risking Gina's displeasure by persuading Steve to install a pool table at the pub. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy and Robin find a rare plant in the Ropers' garden. Comedy, with Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: As the day of his wedding finally arrives, Nick decides to avoid the hurly-burly of the police house by taking a soothing drive in the country - where he promptly gets involved in a domestic dispute and ends up stranded on the moors miles from anywhere

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris returns to work, but Sean is not entirely pleased to see him. Ashley tells Gavin he knows what he has been up to. Betty's past returns to haunt her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice goes in search of Gavin when he disappears from the bar, but she is unprepared for what she finds. Kelly attempts to reveal her secret to Scott

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona asks Audrey if she wants to buy the Salon. Kevin confides in Martin that he loves and hates Sally. Gail suggests to Roy that Hayley might not want to live on her own

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey urges Fred to invest in the salon. Jack loses a lot of money betting on the greyhounds. Hayley is thrilled when Roy asks her to move in with him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The residents celebrate Christmas 1919 with a lavish party, but tensions are rife and Bates's arrest casts a shadow over the festivities. Mary has to consider her future with Sir Richard Carlisle following a disagreement at a shoot and Violet has concerns about Rosamund, whose new suitor - the dashing but raffish Lord Hepworth - is not all he seems. One-off festive special of the hit costume drama, guest starring Nigel Havers and Sharon Small, with Brendan Coyle, Michelle Dockery, Iain Glen, Maggie Smith and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of Midsomer resident and biscuit factory boss Eric Calder leads Barnaby and Nelson to Copenhagen. The pair join forces with local detectives Birgitte Poulsen and Anna Degn in the hunt for the killer, and during the course of their investigation they discover the businessman had numerous links to the capital. Anglo-Danish collaboration originally shown to mark Midsomer's 100th episode, guest starring Ann Eleonora Jorgensen (The Killing), Birgitte Hjort Sorensen (Borgen), Sanjeev Bhaskar and Joanna Scanlan, alongside Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: At the Luxton Deeping Crime Festival, the unveiling of a newly discovered novel by deceased Midsomer crime writer George Summersbee is jeopardised when the manuscript is stolen and local artist Suzie Colebrooke is electrocuted by a booby-trapped roulette wheel. Can new father Barnaby untangle a web of jealousy and obsession to find the killer? With Fiona Dolman, Tamzin Malleson, Liberty Mills, Una Stubbs and Georgia Taylor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a pub landlady is crushed to death by a giant box during a magic show organised by famous illusionist Gideon Latimer, pathologist Kate discovers the cables had been deliberately sabotaged. Evangelising curate Andrew Maplin claims the tragedy is God's vengeance for the pagan traditions still held deep in Midsomer Oaks, but Barnaby and Nelson fear Gideon was the real target. Neil Dudgeon, Gwilym Lee and Tamzin Malleson star in the murder mystery, with Joe Absolom, Andrew-Lee Potts, Justin Salinger, Amanda Burton and Stephanie Leonidas

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: It's the day of the Little Crosby Folk Festival and organiser Toby Winning is found dead, having drowned in a bowl of eggs and live eels. The murder seems to have been inspired by a ballad made famous by the late, lamented star Johnny Carver. Then it comes to light the victim recently announced plans to move the event to a new location, which would have cost local entrepreneur Frank Wainwright dearly. Toby's estranged wife Alice also comes under suspicion, before two further killings take place, echoing more lyrics from the song

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Two-part drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and based on the novel by Peter Robinson. DCI Alan Banks investigates the most difficult case of his life when five girls go missing but only four bodies are found. Chief suspect Marcus Payne is in a coma, and ambitious DS Annie Cabbot from the Professional Standards department pursues Banks in an effort to find out why. Andrea Lowe and Charlotte Riley also star

programme.title: DCI Banks: Aftermath
programme.desc: Conclusion. The mysterious death of serial killer Marcus Payne makes the task of finding his accomplice more difficult and holds up the search for Leanne Wray. With time against him and DS Annie Cabbot breathing down his neck, Banks follows a hunch and takes Payne's wife in for questioning on suspicion of involvement with the rape and murder of the four girls. The lead she provides is more startling than anything the detectives are prepared for. Drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Charlotte Riley and Andrea Lowe

programme.desc: Sally feels she cannot cope without the girls. Vera and Jack are furious when Alec docks their rent from their wages. Jim urges Fiona not to cast Steve away over just one mistake

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A local gamekeeper is injured in a shooting incident on the moors and suspicion falls on his disgruntled predecessor, whose 11-year-old daughter provides damaging testimony. Elsewhere, Maggie discovers her ex-husband has started work at the hospital and Greengrass schemes away as usual, risking Gina's displeasure by persuading Steve to install a pool table at the pub. Starring Nick Berry and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid agrees to buy Mike whatever he wants for his birthday - but is shocked by his request. Vintage comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: When a member of Oxford University's examinations board is found dead, Morse deduces he must have overheard a conversation someone wanted to keep secret at all costs. However, his theory has one major flaw - the victim was deaf. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Barbara Flynn, Michael Gough, Clive Swift, Roger Lloyd Pack and Frederick Treves

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: As the day of his wedding finally arrives, Nick decides to avoid the hurly-burly of the police house by taking a soothing drive in the country - where he promptly gets involved in a domestic dispute and ends up stranded on the moors miles from anywhere

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Chris returns to work, but Sean is not entirely pleased to see him. Ashley tells Gavin he knows what he has been up to. Betty's past returns to haunt her

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Bernice goes in search of Gavin when he disappears from the bar, but she is unprepared for what she finds. Kelly attempts to reveal her secret to Scott

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona asks Audrey if she wants to buy the Salon. Kevin confides in Martin that he loves and hates Sally. Gail suggests to Roy that Hayley might not want to live on her own

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey urges Fred to invest in the salon. Jack loses a lot of money betting on the greyhounds. Hayley is thrilled when Roy asks her to move in with him

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The launch of the latest sparkling wine by Midsomer Vinae Winery falls flat when critic Nadia Simons gives it a scathing review, then guests sampling the vintage start to collapse. Owner William Carnarvon suspects the Farmers' Wives Association of trying to destroy his business, while his wife Diana frets over her mother Matilda, one of the victims. Pathologist Kate reveals the drink was laced with slug poison, but how is the attack connected to the death of a child in a hit-and-run? Barnaby and Nelson are called in to investigate. Neil Dudgeon and Gwilym Lee star in the mystery drama, with Claire Bloom, Mark Bonnar, Rosie Cavaliero and Ruth Gemmell

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The residents celebrate Christmas 1919 with a lavish party, but tensions are rife and Bates's arrest casts a shadow over the festivities. Mary has to consider her future with Sir Richard Carlisle following a disagreement at a shoot and Violet has concerns about Rosamund, whose new suitor - the dashing but raffish Lord Hepworth - is not all he seems. One-off festive special of the hit costume drama, guest starring Nigel Havers and Sharon Small, with Brendan Coyle, Michelle Dockery, Iain Glen, Maggie Smith and Samantha Bond

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective and sidekick Annie Cabbot investigate a murder on a canal boat destroyed in a fire and evidence quickly points to the victim being involved in an art forgery scam. But with a second body discovered, confusion mounts as to who was the intended victim. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, Andrea Lowe, Lorraine Burroughs and Jack Deam

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. When Tom McMahon becomes the third victim of a spate of suspicious fires, Banks realises he is now on the trail of a serial killer. A new discovery about the methods used by the murderer casts doubt on the alibis of all his suspects and the detective is determined to get to the truth at the Asperns' house - despite being warned by his superiors to stay away from the family. Stephen Tompkinson stars

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective investigates a murder in the vicinity of a quiet country church, whose previous vicar committed suicide. As the case proceeds, he uncovers a sinister nest of evil-doing. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Michael Hordern, Angela Morant and Maurice O'Connell

programme.title: Ghostbusters (1984)
programme.desc: Three eccentric scientists put their hi-tech gadgetry and knowledge of the paranormal to money-making use by setting up in business as freelance ghost catchers. As the spook-hunting team members deal with an attempt to shut their operation down, they discover an evil demonic entity is being summoned into existence and it falls to them to stop this creature from destroying the world. programme.title: Ghostbusters (1984)
programme.desc: Three eccentric scientists put their hi-tech gadgetry and knowledge of the paranormal to money-making use by setting up in business as freelance ghost catchers. A musician calls them in to investigate when she sees strange creatures in her kitchen, only to end up possessed. As the spook-hunting team members deal with an attempt to shut their operation down, they discover an evil demonic entity is being summoned into existence and it falls to them to stop this creature from destroying the world. Supernatural comedy adventure, starring Bill Murray, Dan Aykroyd, Harold Ramis, Sigourney Weaver, Ernie Hudson and Rick Moranis

Documentary exploring people's relationship with money, in which the actor, writer and director travels the country to meet people at both extremes of the wealth spectrum. In the first episode, she reunited with former comedy partners Harry Enfield and Paul Whitehouse to discuss creating characters defined by wealth and status, and meets a wealthy businessman who grew up in poverty as a Romany gypsy,

programme.title: Sex Party Secrets
programme.desc: As the popularity of exclusive arranged orgies rises, this documentary explores the hidden world of the UK's self-proclaimed 'sexual elite', providing an insight into the lives of the most selective party planners and their meticulously vetted clients. As the guests sip champagne and fulfil their wildest fantasies behind the doors of multimillion-pound mansions and country estates, the programme explores the motivation behind the desire that drives married couples, young single friends and seasoned sex addicts to seek fleeting intimate experiences and group encounters with strangers

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in New Jersey to help Rose Leone, who spent two years in a coma and came out of hospital to learn her son Michael had run her once-thriving Italian restaurant into the ground. programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Contestants from Cardiff and Newport compete to host the best dinner party and win £1,000 cash prize. programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: The patisserie chefs face chocolate week, beginning with a sharing dessert using both chocolate and a favourite ingredient of judge Cherish Finden. Presented by Tom Allen and Liam Charles
